LAW AND MOTION 302, 2 - DEFENDANTS DOUGLAS MOX AND CHIPS BRAND, LLC.S MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. IN ITS DISCRETION, THE COURT CAN EXCISE AN IMPROPER MATTER "AT ANY TIME." (CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 436.) HERE, PLAINTIFF'S REFERENCE TO CORPORATIONS CODE SECTION 25503 IS IMPROPER. THAT PROVISION PROVIDES A RIGHT OF ACTION TO ENFORCE A VIOLATION OF CORPORATIONS CODE SECTION 25110. THE COURT ALREADY CONCLUDED THAT THE CLAIM UNDER CORPORATIONS CODE SECTION 25110 IS UNTIMELY. THEREFORE, THE REFERENCE TO CORPORATIONS CODE SECTION 25503 IS IMPROPER. THE COURT NOTES THAT PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION DOES NOT ADDRESS THIS ISSUE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN; CLERK: M. MACADANGDANG; NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-02-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Yasmin Bahl's motion to file the second amended complaint is granted.

There is a liberal policy in favor of amendment. (See Nestle v. Santa Monica (1972) 6 Cal.3d 920, 939.) Code of Civil Procedure section 576 states that "[a]ny judge, at any time before or after commencement of trial, in the furtherance of justice, and upon such terms as may be proper, may allow the amendment of any pleading or pretrial conference order." Because judicial policy favors resolution of all disputed matters between the parties in the same lawsuit, the court's discretion will usually be exercised liberally to permit amendment of the pleadings. (Edmon & Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial 6:638 (Rutter 2023); see also Nestle v. Santa Monica (1972) 6 Cal.3d 920, 939.) Indeed, "it is an abuse of discretion to deny leave to amend where the opposing party was not misled or prejudiced by the amendment." (Kittredge Sports Co. v. Superior Court (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.)

Here, there is no trial date, the amendments do not change the tenor of this case, and defendants fail to show prejudice from permitting the filing of the second amended complaint. Issues regarding the merits of any amendment can be raised and addressed by post-amendment motion.

Plaintiff to file the second amended complaint within 10 days of notice of entry of this order.

Order is Signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Off Calendar Jan-16-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. JOSE A. CORDOVA, Esq. of Law Office of Jose A. Cordova (tel: 415-466-8211) for Plaintiff.
Atty. JIMMY CZODLI, Esq. and Atty. SCOTT MENGER, Esq. of Carlton Fields, LLP (tel: 310-843-6300) for Defendants Douglas M. Mox, 1 of 1 Media, LLC and Chips Brand, LLC.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS DOUGLAS MOX, AND CHIPS BRAND, LLC'S NOTICE OF DEMURRER AND DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The court adopts its tentative ruling as modified:

Defendant Mox and Chips Brand, LLC's demurrer to all six causes of action is overruled as to the first, second, third, and fourth causes of action and sustained with leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff may file an amended complaint no later than February 13, 2025. Plaintiff, Yasmin Bahl, is suing Douglas M. Mox for fraud after she invested over a million dollars in his company, and never received the money back or any interest she was promised. Plaintiff is also suing the businesses she invested in. Specifically, Plaintiff alleges causes of action for (1) fraud, (2) negligent misrepresentation, (3) omission of facts, (4) violations of the Corporations Code sections 25501 and 25503, (5) violation of Corporations Code section 25110, (6) quantum meruit/unjust enrichment. Plaintiff requests return of the money that she invested in the company, interest, and attorney's fees and costs. The fifth cause of action was brought after the expiration of the applicable two-year statute of limitation and is time barred. Defendants' argument that the statute of limitations bars plaintiff's fourth cause of action is not amenable on this pleading motion since the face of the complaint fails to show that Plaintiff's fourth cause of action is necessarily time-barred. Defendants' argument that the complaint fails to join a necessary party lacks merit because the face of the complaint does not establish that a necessary party is missing. The third cause of action for "omission of facts" adequately alleges a cause of action for fraud by failure to disclose. The sixth cause of action fails to allege the required element that a third party received property that a defendant knew or had reason to know was fraudulently obtained. (CACI 375.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR# 13293 (tel: 415-274-9977 / email: calendar-LA@veritext.com).
